  • Devtoberfest Community Page to Join/Register: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest

Five years of CPILint:

  • Five years of CPILint:
     https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/five-years-of-cpilint/ba-p/13812638
  • Release of version 1.0.5:
     https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/cpilint-version-1-0-5-is-here/ba-p/13810431
  • Devtoberfest session - 🔴 Automate your SAP Cloud Integration governance with CPILint:
     https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/automate-your-sap-cloud-integration-governance-with-cpilint/ev-p/13802890

ML and AI learning content

  • Leveraging Image Embedding and HANA Vector Engine for Reverse Product Search: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/leveraging-image-embedding-and-hana-vector-engine-for-reverse-product/ba-p/13798320
  • How Metadata Enrichment can enhance SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine on GenAI scenarios: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-metadata-enrichment-can-enhance-sap-hana-cloud-vector-engine-on-genai/ba-p/13662543
  • “Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core” CodeJam in Munich: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/getting-started-with-generative-ai-hub-on-sap-ai-core/ev-p/13854907
  • "Getting Started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA" as an SAP CodeJam topic: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/quot-getting-started-with-machine-learning-using-sap-hana-quot-as-a-new-sap/ba-p/13574098
  • Machine Learning with SAP HANA Cloud: a time-series forecasting example: https://community.sap.com/t5/artificial-intelligence-and-machine-learning-blogs/hands-on-tutorial-machine-learning-with-sap-hana-cloud/ba-p/13683430

SAP BTP Solution Diagrams shape library in draw.io

  • New built-in SAP shape library for BTP solution diagrams in draw.io: https://www.drawio.com/blog/sap-diagrams

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 Devtoberfest Promo
2:23 Five years of CPILint
3:17 ML and AI learning content
5:35 SAP BTP Solution Diagrams shape library in draw.io

TRANSCRIPT

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for September 5th, 2024.

[Josh] Hey everybody, it is September-October and that time period reminds me of a certain festival. That's right, Devtoberfest. Devtoberfest, an SAP TechEd event is here. We're putting it on and it starts in two weeks. So the first event is going to happen September 23rd, so please register. It's easy to register, you just join the SAP group. I'll put a link to that community page. And if you're already a member of that community page group, you're already enrolled to participate in Devtoberfest this year. And you can earn points.

And speaking of points, we have that four level game board, you can reach Nerdvana, and you qualify to be in our grand prize drawing. Like years past, people have gone on trips to major theme parks around the world as part of Devtoberfest. So by earning points and learning, you actually get a prize, possibly. So go to that community page and click join.

The other thing I wanted to point out is the number five. It's the fifth year I've had a blue beard, that I've been your blue bearded idiot. But hey, it's also the fifth Devtoberfest. Can you believe it? We've been doing this for developers, by developers for five years, but it's not just for developers, right? Anybody who wants to learn as part of the SAP community can pick a topic they're interested in and they can just complete tutorials or attend events and watch an SAP expert present to them. So five days of the week, five different topics.

Speaking of that, if you look at the calendar in our SAP community page, You'll see Monday has green circles for ABAP and CAP. Tuesday is tooling Tuesdays with a purple circle. That's fun to say. Wednesday we have red circles for integration. Thursday, don't be mad, machine learning AI and data with a blue circle. And then we have front end Fridays with an orange circle. So really, two weeks away, four great weeks of content, but Devtoberfest is five weeks long this year because we are going to stop and do SAP TechEd October 8th and 9th live from Germany. So the developer advocates, a lot of SAP experts will be doing virtual tech ed live during that week of October 8th and 9th. And then we come right back for week three of SAP Devtoberfest on October 14th. So please register. Let's pump up those numbers. Let's do as many tutorials as possible and see how many people can qualify for Nerdvana. Can't wait to see you online. Bye.

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. Look what I got over the mail. Yep, a sticker of CPILint CPILint, which is an open-source tool that can help you with your Cloud Integration governance, just turned five years old. Thanks, Morten, for all the great work. And well, to celebrate it, Morten just released a new version of the tool, version 1.0.5, which now supports OAuth 2.0 to communicate with your cloud integration tenant, a mechanism to import a rules file, in a new rule to check user roles. If you want to learn more about CPI-Lint, make sure to check out the tutorial session that we have scheduled for the 25th of September, where Morten will be sharing with us how to automate our SAP Cloud Integration governance with CPI-Lint. Hasta la vista, SAP developers.

[Witalij] You might have heard about SAP HANA Cloud's new capability called the vector engine a lot this year. You might have even participated in the developer challenge, which I organized earlier this year and where we used it as well. Now at the SAP community, you will find two new blog posts describing interesting cases. So one is how metadata enrichment can enhance SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine. And the other one is how leveraging image embeddings and SAP HANA vector engine for reverse product search can be implemented.

If you are joining the new SAP CodeJam topic that my teammate Nora announced last week on getting started with the Generative AI Hub in SAP AI Core, then you would use this capability as well. We have two of these CodeJams already announced, and they are available for you to sign up if you are in Warsaw or in Munich.

Getting started with machine learning using SAP HANA was quite a popular topic for code trends during the first half of this year. But even now we have already four more events scheduled for you. So if you are in Isernhagen or in Stuttgart in Germany, or if you are in Porto or Lisbon in Portugal, then please join me this month if you're interested in some hands-on exercises where you will be using SAP Business Application Studio, Python, and Predictive Analytics Library from SAP HANA Cloud to train some classification models. The other kind of model that are extremely popular among SAP customers are time series forecasting.

And my colleagues, Andreas Foster and Yannick Schaper, published recently a blog post describing how to do that, where they're focusing on one of the most powerful time series forecasting algorithms. And they're showing how to build both single and multiple time series.

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. It is easier than ever to get started with SAP BTP solution diagrams. Why is that, you might ask? Well, the icons are now available out of the box in draw.io. All you have to do is click on More Shapes at the bottom of the Shapes panel in draw.io, enable the checkbox next to the SAP Shape library in the Networking section, and click Apply. Voila. All the icons are now available there for you to start your diagrams. I'm looking forward to seeing your architecture diagrams in SAP Community. Happy diagramming.

entry.title: Unlocking SAP BTP: Your Guide to SAP Business Technology Platform Use Case One-Pagers
entry.content:
SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) offers a unified environment that allows businesses to bring together application development, automation, data and analytics, integration, and AI capabilities, all optimized for SAP applications. However, with such a suite of tools, knowing where to start with SAP BTP can be the most challenging part for businesses. To support organizations in their digital transformation journey and help customers unlock the full potential of SAP BTP, we created SAP BTP Use Case One-Pagers.  

 

Understanding SAP BTP Use Case One-Pagers

The SAP BTP Use Case One-Pagers serves as an effective compass for businesses. It explains the business values, features, and implementation strategies of various components within the SAP BTP ecosystem. The main goal of these one-pagers is to simplify the complexities of digital solutions and streamline your journey toward full adoption and utilization of the SAP BTP portfolio.

The SAP BTP Use Case One-Pagers also highlights the proven and validated use cases that customers should look to first. Examples of measurable business value with targeted solutions to business challenges show the potential outcomes of SAP BTP utilization.

 

How to utilize SAP BTP Use Case One-Pagers:

With the SAP BTP Use Case One-Pagers at your disposal, your first step is to identify the business challenge or growth area you want to address. This could range from enhancing customer experiences to improving decision-making capabilities with real-time data. You could also drill down to your line of business/department area to see what use cases are available. Consider a business that is currently utilizing SAP S/4HANA for Finance.

  1. In this scenario, you can start with the SAP S/4HANA Finance section of the One-Pager, and read through the business challenges each use case targets. 
  2. Let’s say you know that your company is having a tough time with manual processes that take time, and some manual document processes are especially prone to errors. The 'Automate processes to empower finance professionals' use case provides a clear insight into the capabilities of SAP BTP connecting SAP S/4HANA to various applications and processes.

    • SAP Integration Suite can seamlessly integrate finance with sales and HR, while SAP Build Process Automation fosters an automated, cohesive workflow to create a streamlined and unified workflow that enhances efficiency and productivity.

  3. You now understand the expected outcomes and the value the use case will bring to your business, with a step-by-step implementation guide from the corresponding Discovery Center Missions. The Mission shows the reference architecture, practical guidance for implementing the use case, and business accelerators to help ensure a smooth implementation.

  4. You can also read further about other existing customers that are deriving business value with SAP BTP, or take the Learning course. 

 

Begin leveraging these resources today to unlock the full potential of the SAP Business Technology Platform, foster business innovation, and accelerate growth. So why wait? Immerse yourself in these invaluable guides now and embark on a transformative journey with SAP BTP Use Case One-Pagers today!

entry.title: Step by step setup JDBC PostgreSQL connection in SAP BTP Integration Suite (part 2)
entry.content:
Part 1

Now we will setup JDBC connection to our virtual host that we already defined before. Go to Monitor in your SAP BTP Integration Suite --> Integrations and APIs --> Manage Security tab --> JDBC Material.

1. Click ‘Add’ to adding JDBC data resource.

2. Let fill the parameters

 

Name: PosgreTest

Description: Connection to PosgreSQL

User: admin_db

Password: [fill with your own db pwd]

Then we click ‘Deploy’

entry.title: SAP Cloud Integration: HTTP vs. OData Receiver Adapter
entry.content:
Introduction

In SAP Cloud Integration, receiver adapters are crucial for enabling communication with external systems. When integrating with an OData API, developers can choose to use either an OData (V2/V4) receiver adapter or an HTTP receiver adapter. While the OData adapter offers convenience by automating some tasks, it also comes with limitations that may necessitate the use of an HTTP adapter. In this blog, I will share my experiences regarding the advantages and limitations of the OData adapter in comparison to the HTTP adapter.

Advantages of the OData Receiver Adapter

Automated X-CSRF-Token & Cookie Handling

One of the key advantages of the OData adapter is its automatic handling of the X-CSRF-Token and cookies, which is essential for making POST, PUT, and DELETE requests. When making a POST, PUT, or DELETE request using the HTTP receiver adapter without providing a valid token and cookie, you will likely encounter the error...: “HTTP operation failed invoking … with statusCode: 403” with the response body “CSRF token validation failed”. (Important to note is that - although this error seems to indicate a missing or invalid X-CSRF-Token - it may also be caused by missing or invalid cookies).

In order to resolve this error, you must manually retrieve the X-CSRF-Token through a separate call and ensure that both the token and the cookie header are reused for subsequent requests. On the contrary, the OData adapter automatically takes care of both the X-CSRF-Token and the cookies and thereby eliminates the need for these manual steps.

Built-in Tool to Facilitate the Development Process

Another advantage of the OData adapter is the intuitive tool that it provides for entity selection and query options such as filtering and sorting. This tool simplifies the development process and reduces the likelihood of errors.

Additionally, this tool offers a feature to automatically generate an XSD file containing the required payload structure for the request, which is particularly useful for message mapping.

Straightforward XML Structure

Another significant difference between the OData and HTTP adapters related to the way in which the XML message body needs to be structured before sending a request. The HTTP adapter requires the payload to be formatted exactly as the receiver expects, including proper namespaces, xml version and encoding. In contrast, with the OData adapter, the message body needs to be defined in a straightforward structure, as shown below:

 

<EntitySetName>
  <EntityTypeName>
    <Property1></Property1>
    <Property2></Property2>
    <Property3></Property3>
    <!-- etc. -->
  </EntityTypeName>
</EntitySetName>

 

The OData adapter then takes care of sending the payload in the required structure.

As an example, if you want to create a supplier invoice using the "API_SUPPLIERINVOICE_PROCESS_SRV" service, you can define the message body in the following way:

 

<A_SupplierInvoice>
	<A_SupplierInvoiceType>
		<CompanyCode></CompanyCode>
		<DocumentDate></DocumentDate>
		<PostingDate></PostingDate>
        <!-- etc. -->
	</A_SupplierInvoiceType>
</A_SupplierInvoice>

 

However, the resulting request will be structured as follows:

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<entry xml:base="https://xxxxxxxxxx/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SUPPLIERINVOICE_PROCESS_SRV/" xmlns:d="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices" xmlns:m="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom">
<content type="application/xml">
<m:properties>
			<d:CompanyCode></d:CompanyCode>
			<d:DocumentDate></d:DocumentDate>
			<d:PostingDate></d:PostingDate>
                <!-- etc. -->
		</m:properties>
	</content>
</entry>

 

So, the following request from Cloud Integration:

will be received as follows:

In other words, the OData adapter generally involves much less manual work compared to the HTTP adapter. However, there are specific cases in which using the HTTP adapter is necessary, even though the endpoint is an OData service.

Limitations of the OData Receiver Adapter

XML-only Payload

One of the main limitations of the OData adapter is that it only supports XML message bodies. Attempting to use the OData adapter with non-XML payloads results in the following error: “org.xmlpull.v1.XmlPullParserException: only whitespace content allowed before start tag and not ...”.

In many cases, this can easily be resolved by converting the message to XML format, such as by using a JSON-to-XML converter.

However, in some specific cases, a non-XML message body is inevitable. This is for example the case if you want to post an attachment using the "AttachmentContentSet" entity of the "API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV" service, which requires the payload to be a binary string. Attempting to send an XML body will lead to the following error: "com.sap.gateway.core.ip.component.odata.exception.OsciException: Bad Request : 400 : HTTP/1.1". In this case, the HTTP adapter provides the necessary flexibility regarding content-types.

No Support for Navigation Paths

Another limitation of the OData adapter is that it does not support navigation entities in the resource path, which can lead to errors like “...UriNotMatchingException: Could not match segment: …”. For example, retrieving bank data from a business partner using “A_BusinessPartner('{BusinessPartner}')/to_BusinessPartnerBank” of the API_BUSINESS_PARTNER service is not possible with the OData adapter.

Fortunately, as long as you only need to obtain data using a GET request, the HTTP adapter is fairly straightforward to use, because it usually does not require an X-CSRF-Token and cookie. However, if you need to create, update or delete data, a few more steps are needed to manually retrieve and set the X-CSRF-Token and cookie.

Conclusion

The considerations for choosing between the HTTP and OData receiver adapters in SAP Cloud Integration depend on your integration scenario's specific requirements. The OData adapter simplifies integration with automated token handling and entity management, making it ideal for standard SAP services with XML payloads. However, for specific scenarios that require non-XML payloads or navigation paths, the HTTP adapter provides the necessary flexibility.

I hope this blog provides some insights into when and how to use the HTTP and OData adapters effectively. Please feel free to share any additional experiences or tips in the comments.

Summary Table

Feature

HTTP Adapter

OData Adapter

Automated X-CSRF-Token & Cookie Handling

No

Yes

Built-in Tool to Facilitate the Development Process

No

Yes

Automatic .xsd File Generation

No

Yes

Automatically Takes Care of Namespaces

No

Yes

Allows Non-XML Payload

Yes

No

Allows Navigation in Resource Path

Yes

No

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Migration: Insights and Guidance for IT Leaders
entry.content:
As organizations worldwide continue to navigate the complexities of digital transformation, many that still rely on SAP ERP or the on-premises version of SAP S/4HANA are considering a major change: migrating to the cloud. This move isn’t just about a technical upgrade; it’s a strategic decision that could shape the future of your business. To help you get started, we’ve completely updated our guide, Mapping Your Journey to SAP S/4HANA, with practical insights, step-by-step instructions, best practices, and detailed background information.

Keeping up with the fast pace of digital change isn’t just beneficial — it’s essential. Industries like automotive, with their complex supply chains, clearly demonstrate the risks of not adapting quickly. That’s exactly why we created the original guide back in 2018, which quickly became a go-to resource for many companies. Now, I’m excited to share that we’ve just released a fully updated edition.

This edition brings together insights from dozens of SAP experts across the company, each sharing their unique perspectives and experiences. Inside, you’ll find a comprehensive guide that covers every aspect of the transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. We’ve focused on the key technology elements to help IT decision-makers confidently plan their roadmap, avoid common pitfalls, and ensure a smooth path to a successful go-live.

What makes this guide special is the wealth of knowledge it offers — drawn from thousands of successful transformation projects. It’s more than just a collection of tips; it’s packed with real-world experience, brought to life with clear text and visuals. Since every project is unique, we’ve included plenty of links to additional resources so you can rely on this guide at every step of your migration journey. The feedback from our customers speaks for itself: the guide has been downloaded more than 180,000 times since it was first published.

Why an update to our key reference

You may be wondering why a new edition, and why now? As one of the co-authors, I can tell you that while we’ve always kept the guide up to date, the past six years have seen major changes in the SAP landscape. New tools and methods have emerged, and our RISE with SAP offering has grown significantly. These developments have changed the way we help customers move to Cloud ERP, so it’s the perfect time to update the guide with these innovations and the valuable feedback we’ve received from our customers and partners.

Our goal with this guide remains the same: to give IT leaders everything they need in one place to successfully implement SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. We've carefully organized the guide into three key chapters.

1. Strategic decisions

In the first chapter, we’ll help you make smart choices about the transition. We cover everything from deciding between public and private cloud options to understanding the unique benefits of the GROW with SAP and RISE with SAP programs, and even navigating the complexities of cloud operations.

2. Ingredients for project success

The middle section delves into the critical factors that determine the success of your project. We discuss change management, knowledge management, customer and supplier master data integration, the partner ecosystem, and more.

3. Essential tools from SAP

The final chapter provides an in-depth look at the 15 most important tools available to help you assess your systems, plan and execute the migration, and track project progress. These include SAP Data Services, SAP Readiness Check, ABAP development tools, and the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit.

More than just a guide, Mapping Your Journey to SAP S/4HANA is your trusted companion as an IT leader navigating a complex transformation. Whether you’re diving into the theory or need practical tips to keep your organization on track beyond 2027, this guide has you covered. You can read it cover to cover for a complete understanding or use it as a quick reference for specific stages or challenges.

I invite you to download it today and take the next step in your digital transformation journey: Download now
